checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=1537823.78 (2011 annual) -> SalesRevenueGoodsNet=9556911.18 (2012 annual) -> SalesRevenueGoodsNet=29597324.11 (2013 annual)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=224037.67 (2010-12-31); OtherAssetsNoncurrent=23134.32 (2011-09-30); no further OtherAssetsNoncurrent data reported for 2012 or 2013
Assets=48518766.30 (2013-09-30) jumping to Assets=112136940.88 (2013-12-31) with no intangible/goodwill breakdown provided

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K income statement and cash flow statement
10-K income statement and cash flow statement
10-K income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=2348742.73 (2013-12-31) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=29597324.11 (2013 annual); InventoryNet growth trails revenue growth

CostOfGoodsSold=8104319.13 (2013 annual) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=29597324.11 (2013 annual) implies ~72.6% gross margin, up from CostOfGoodsSold=646543.49 (2011 annual) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=1537823.78 (2011 annual) ~58% margin, consistent with scaling operations

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic swung from 18132439.97 (2011 annual) to -13472699.99 (2012 annual) to 50698263.18 (2013 annual)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet, allowance and AR line items

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=94972.49 (2013-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=9082048.73 (2013-12-31), a large step-up after two quarters of zero reserve

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: DeferredRevenueCurrent data ends at 4058247.54 (2011-12-31) with no values reported for 2012 or 2013
GrossProfit and CostOfRevenue series are sparsely populated (last consistent GrossProfit data point is 913197.02 (2012-01-01 to 2012-03-31)), limiting continuous margin analysis through 2013

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A large non-operating/non-recurring gain (e.g., bargain purchase gain on acquisition, gain on debt/derivative settlement, or asset revaluation) appears to have been booked in Q4 2013, inflating net income far beyond operating performance and coinciding with a near-doubling of the balance sheet in a single quarter, which is not reflected in operating cash flow.

rationale_evidence.quote: Assets=48518766.30 (2013-09-30) increasing to Assets=112136940.88 (2013-12-31); StockholdersEquity=38663544.10 (2013-09-30) increasing to StockholdersEquity=98563180.42 (2013-12-31)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Persistent, multi-year divergence between reported net income and operating cash flow suggests possible premature revenue recognition or aggressive accrual/timing choices that inflate GAAP earnings relative to cash generated by the business.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K income statement and cash flow statement
10-K income statement and cash flow statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad debt allowance was reduced to zero for two consecutive quarters while receivables continued to grow to multi-million dollar balances, then was abruptly increased at year-end, consistent with under-reserving during interim periods to support reported receivable and income levels, followed by a catch-up adjustment.

top_signals: Large Q4 2013 net income of NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=60643587.49 vastly exceeding OperatingIncomeLoss=1803259.71 for the same quarter, coinciding with Assets nearly doubling from 48518766.30 (2013-09-30) to 112136940.88 (2013-12-31)

Persistent multi-year divergence between positive net income and deeply negative operating cash flow (e.g., NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=50698263.18 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-16156281.62 for 2013 annual)
Allowance for doubtful accounts dropped to 0.0 for two consecutive quarters in 2013 despite AR balances of 7.8-8.2 million, then jumped to 94972.49 at year end
NT 10-K filed 2014-03-17 immediately preceding the delayed 10-K filed 2014-03-31, indicating late/irregular filing chronology
